channel.title: LLMs on Zhixuan's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in LLMs on Zhixuan's Blog

item.title: Reading List 2026
item.description:
Deepseek V4

  • DeepSeek-V4: Towards Highly Efficient Million-Token Context Intelligence ✅
  • mHC: Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections ✅
  • DSpark: Confidence-Scheduled Speculative Decoding with Semi-Autoregressive Generation ✅
  • DFlash: Block Diffusion for Flash Speculative Decoding ✅

GLM 5.2

  • GLM-5: from Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering ✅
  • GLM-5.2: Built for Long-Horizon Tasks ✅
  • IndexShare ✅

Others

  • Lil’Log: Scaling Laws, Carefully
  • On-Policy Distillation ✅
  • mini-sglang

vLLM

  • Inside vLLM: Anatomy of a High-Throughput LLM Inference System ⭐️ ✅
  • 透过 vLLM 深入分析一次推理的完整旅程：从 HTTP 请求到 Token 流出
  • Async scheduling: PR 29957 & 32951
  • vLLM Mooncake KV Connector详解：让KV Cache在GPU之间“瞬移”

item.title: Scheduler and KV transfer in disaggregated prefill
item.description:
1. Scheduler

Scheduler (vllm/v1/core/sched/scheduler.py) is the central resource allocator for vLLM’s v1 engine. Each iteration it decides which requests run, how many tokens each gets, and which KV cache blocks to (re-)use. It owns two queues — self.waiting and self.running — plus a KVCacheManager for block allocation. The scheduler has no notion of a distinct “prefill phase” or “decode phase”; every request is simply tracked by num_computed_tokens and num_tokens_with_spec, and the scheduler’s job is to close the gap between the two.
